label: OpenMetadata
description: Open-source metadata management platform. Centralise data discovery, lineage, data quality and governance across your Hadoop and cloud-native data stack.

endpoints.label: OpenMetadata UI
endpoints.description: Data discovery, lineage and governance portal

bindings.name: ingress-setup
bindings._description: Writes the canonical ingress shape every KDPS chart accepts. The `hosts[]` entry uses the rich object form `{host, paths[]}` so OpenMetadata- and Trino-style charts work out of the box; the Superset chart's template was made shape-tolerant (accepts string OR object) in chart >= 0.12.25.

bindings.name: ingress-nginx-tuning
bindings._description: Backslash-escaped dots in the leaf key — the UI binding engine (bindings.ts:pathToParts) treats backslash-dot as a literal dot so the rendered values look like `ingress.annotations: { "nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-buffer-size": "..." }`.

form.name: namespace
form.label: Namespace Kubernetes
form.validationHint: Namespace too long — the Kerberos principal (oma-ing-<namespace>) would exceed the 32-character IPA limit.

fields.name: security.adminUsers
fields.label: Admin users (comma-separated)
fields.help: List of usernames granted the Admin role (e.g. admin, john.doe). These map to AUTHORIZER_ADMIN_PRINCIPALS.

fields.name: security.adminGroups
fields.label: Admin groups (comma-separated)
fields.help: IdP group names (OIDC/LDAP) to map to the Admin role.

fields.name: security.principalDomain
fields.label: Admin email domain
fields.help: Email domain used when bootstrap-creating admin user records (sets AUTHORIZER_PRINCIPAL_DOMAIN). Must match the 'email' claim domain your IdP issues for those users (e.g. dev21.hadoop.clemlab.com). Pre-filled from the selected Security Profile (oidc.principalDomain) — itself bootstrapped from cluster oidc-env. Override here per-deploy if needed. If left blank with no upstream value, OpenMetadata falls back to 'open-metadata.org' — which will cause 'Account already exists' on first OIDC login when your IdP email domain differs.

form.name: authenticationConfig
form.label: Authentication
form.help: Choose how users authenticate to OpenMetadata. 'oidc' requires a Keycloak realm to be configured in Ambari. 'ldap' requires an accessible LDAP/AD directory.

fields.name: global.security.auth.mode
fields.label: Authentication mode
fields.help: Sets AUTHENTICATION_PROVIDER in the OpenMetadata server. Select 'oidc' to register a Keycloak client automatically.

fields.name: global.security.auth.oidc.providerName
fields.label: OIDC provider display name
fields.help: Label shown on the OpenMetadata login button (CUSTOM_OIDC_AUTHENTICATION_PROVIDER_NAME).

fields.name: global.security.auth.oidc.jwksUri
fields.label: JWKS URI override
fields.help: Leave empty for Keycloak — the chart derives the JWKS URI automatically from the issuer URL (<issuerUrl>/protocol/openid-connect/certs). Only set this for non-Keycloak OIDC providers that expose their public keys at a different endpoint.

fields.name: global.security.auth.oidc.userClaim
fields.label: JWT user claim
fields.help: JWT claim used as the OpenMetadata username.

fields.name: global.security.auth.ldap.host
fields.label: LDAP host
fields.help: Hostname of the LDAP/AD server (without ldap:// prefix).

fields.name: global.security.auth.ldap.userBaseDn
fields.label: User search base DN
fields.help: Base DN for user lookup, e.g. ou=people,dc=example,dc=com.

fields.name: global.security.auth.ldap.userSearchFilter
fields.label: User search filter
fields.help: LDAP filter to locate a user. Use {0} as the username placeholder.

fields.name: global.security.auth.ldap.bindDn
fields.label: Bind DN (read account)
fields.help: Service account DN used to search the directory, e.g. cn=svc-om,ou=svc,dc=example,dc=com.

fields.name: global.security.auth.ldap.bindPassword
fields.label: Bind password
fields.help: Password for the bind DN.

fields.name: global.security.auth.ldap.groupBaseDn
fields.label: Group search base DN
fields.help: Base DN for group membership lookups (optional).

fields.name: global.security.auth.ldap.mailAttribute
fields.label: Email attribute
fields.help: LDAP attribute containing the user e-mail address.

fields.name: ui_hive_metastore_uri
fields.label: Service Hive Metastore
fields.help: Select a running Hive Metastore to auto-configure the Hive connector.

fields.name: ui_hive_metastore_uri_override
fields.label: URI Hive Metastore (override)
fields.help: e.g. thrift://hive-metastore.ns:9083 — overrides auto-discovered value.

fields.name: ui_trino_host
fields.label: Trino Coordinator
fields.help: Select a running Trino coordinator to auto-configure the Trino connector.

fields.name: ui_trino_host_override
fields.label: Trino host:port (override)
fields.help: e.g. trino-coordinator.ns:8080 — overrides auto-discovered value.

fields.name: ingestion.enabled
fields.label: Déployer Airflow (ingestion bundlée)
fields.help: Deploy the openmetadata/ingestion Airflow image alongside the server. Disable if you connect to an existing Airflow.

fields.name: ingestion.airflowUrl
fields.label: URL Airflow externe
fields.help: Base URL of an existing Airflow instance (e.g. http://airflow-webserver.airflow:8080). Used when bundled Airflow is disabled.

fields.name: postgresql.storage.size
fields.label: PostgreSQL — Taille PVC
fields.help: Persistent volume size for the PostgreSQL database. Increase for large metadata graphs.

fields.name: postgresql.storage.storageClass
fields.label: PostgreSQL — StorageClass
fields.help: Leave empty to use the cluster default StorageClass.

fields.name: opensearch.storage.size
fields.label: OpenSearch — Taille PVC
fields.help: Persistent volume size for the OpenSearch index.

fields.name: opensearch.storage.storageClass
fields.label: OpenSearch — StorageClass
fields.help: Leave empty to use the cluster default StorageClass.

fields.name: replicaCount
fields.label: Réplicas
fields.help: Scale to ≥ 2 with sticky sessions enabled on the Ingress for HA.

fields.name: monitoring.discovery
fields.label: Discover monitoring stack
fields.help: Auto-fill monitoring namespace and release from installed kube-prometheus-stack.

fields.name: ingress.enabled
fields.label: Enable Ingress
fields.help: Expose the service through a Kubernetes Ingress. Required when an authentication profile (OIDC / LDAP-over-SSL) is selected in Step 1 — OAuth callbacks need a public hostname and HTTPS.

fields.name: ingress.ingressClassName
fields.label: Ingress class
fields.help: Ingress class name (e.g. 'nginx', 'openshift-default'). Leave empty for the default controller.

fields.name: ingress.host
fields.label: Hostname (DNS)
fields.help: Public DNS name the ingress controller will route. e.g. service.example.com.

fields.name: ingress.path
fields.label: HTTP path
fields.help: Base path for the Ingress (usually '/').

fields.name: ingress.tlsMode
fields.label: TLS mode
fields.help: Pick how this service gets its TLS leaf. Recommended path is **Sign via cert-manager** when cert-manager is installed in the cluster (auto-renewal, standard K8s pattern). Fall back to **Sign with Company CA** (if you've uploaded one to the PKI registry) or **Sign with Ambari Internal CA** (testing only, browsers will warn). Options whose backing system isn't installed are hidden automatically. TLS is mandatory when an auth profile is selected in Step 1.

options.label: Source from external Secret store (Vault, ESO)
None (HTTP only — not allowed with auth)

fields.name: ingress.tlsSecret
fields.label: Existing TLS Secret name
fields.help: Name of a kubernetes.io/tls Secret already present in the release namespace. The chart references it via ingress.tls[].secretName.

fields.name: ingress.tlsUpload.cert
fields.label: TLS certificate (PEM, leaf + chain)
fields.help: Paste the full chain (leaf first). Saved as tls.crt in the new Secret.

fields.name: ingress.tlsUpload.key
fields.label: TLS private key (PEM)
fields.help: Paste the private key matching the certificate above. Saved as tls.key.

fields.name: ingress.tlsUpload.secretName
fields.label: Target Secret name
fields.help: Name of the kubernetes.io/tls Secret the view will create from the uploaded cert/key. Leave blank to use the default `<releaseName>-ingress-tls` (computed server-side at deploy time).

fields.name: ingress.tlsSelfSign.caName
fields.label: Company CA
fields.help: Pick a Company Issuing CA from the PKI registry. The view mints a leaf TLS cert signed by this CA before installing the chart. CAs are stored as Kubernetes Secrets in the `ambari-pki` namespace labeled `ambari.clemlab.com/resource-type=issuing-ca`. Upload CAs via Cluster security → Certificate Authorities.

fields.name: ingress.tlsSelfSign.validityDays
fields.label: Leaf validity (days)
fields.help: How long the minted leaf certificate is valid. Capped server-side at 825 (CA/Browser Forum baseline).

fields.name: ingress.certManager.issuerName
fields.label: ClusterIssuer
fields.help: Pick a cert-manager ClusterIssuer or Issuer. The view writes a Certificate resource pre-install; cert-manager mints the Secret and auto-renews it before expiry.

fields.name: ingress.certManager.validityDays
fields.label: Leaf validity (days)
fields.help: Validity in days. Translated to durationHours on the Certificate resource. cert-manager renews at duration/6 before expiry.

fields.name: ingress.externalSecret.secretStoreName
fields.label: SecretStore
fields.help: Pick an external-secrets SecretStore or ClusterSecretStore. Typically Vault-backed for TLS leaves (PKI mount).

fields.name: ingress.externalSecret.remoteKey
fields.label: Remote key / path
fields.help: Path/key in the upstream store. For Vault PKI: 'pki/issue/<role>'. For Vault KV: 'secret/data/tls/<name>'. For AWS Secrets Manager: the secret name.

fields.name: ingress.externalSecret.refreshInterval
fields.label: Refresh interval
fields.help: ESO refresh cadence. e.g. '1h', '15m', '0' (no periodic refresh).

fields.name: ingressTuning
fields.label: Ingress tuning (nginx)

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyBufferSize
fields.label: proxy-buffer-size
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-buffer-size. Leave blank to use chart default (16k under OIDC/OAuth, otherwise nginx default 4k/8k). Bump if you see 502 + 'upstream sent too big header' on OAuth callbacks.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyBuffersNumber
fields.label: proxy-buffers-number
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-buffers-number. Total response buffers = number × size.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyBusyBuffersSize
fields.label: proxy-busy-buffers-size
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-busy-buffers-size. Should be ≥ proxy-buffer-size and ≤ total buffer capacity.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyBodySize
fields.label: proxy-body-size
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size. Maximum request body the proxy accepts (e.g. '64m'). Bump for large uploads / file imports.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyReadTimeout
fields.label: proxy-read-timeout (seconds)
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-read-timeout. Bump for long-running queries (Trino, Superset). Default 60.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyConnectTimeout
fields.label: proxy-connect-timeout (seconds)
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-connect-timeout. Time the proxy waits when opening a connection to the backend pod.

Advanced — usually leave blank. nginx ingress annotations (proxy buffers / timeouts). Chart-side defaults are sensible for the common case; expand if you need to override (large headers on OAuth, long-running queries, big uploads).

fields.name: tls.https.enabled
fields.label: Activer HTTPS interne
fields.help: Enables server-side TLS on the OpenMetadata API port. Ambari auto-generates a PKCS12 keystore signed by the internal CA.

fields.name: tls.https.secretName
fields.label: Secret keystore
fields.help: Leave empty → <release>-https.

fields.name: tls.https.keystoreKey
fields.label: Clé du keystore dans le Secret

form.name: rangerTagSync
form.label: Ranger TagSync
form.help: Wires OpenMetadata as a Ranger TagSync source (RANGER-4978). Tags applied to tables/columns in OpenMetadata are propagated to Ranger and enforced on Trino queries.

fields.name: rangerTagSyncEnabled
fields.label: Activer Ranger TagSync
fields.help: Creates the ranger-tagsync-site ConfigMap consumed by the Ranger TagSync daemon.

fields.name: rangerTagSyncToken
fields.label: OpenMetadata Bot JWT Token
fields.help: JWT token for the OpenMetadata bot account used by Ranger TagSync to call the OM REST API.

fields.name: rangerTagSyncTokenSecretName
fields.label: Secret Kubernetes (token)
fields.help: Name of the K8s Secret that holds the bot token under key 'tagsync-token'. Leave empty to auto-generate from release name.

fields.name: rangerTagSyncEndpoint
fields.label: OpenMetadata API endpoint (override)
fields.help: REST API base URL seen from the Ranger TagSync host, e.g. https://openmetadata.example.com/api. Defaults to https://<ingressHost>/api when Ingress is enabled.

fields.name: rangerTagSyncTrinoServiceFqn
fields.label: OpenMetadata Trino service FQN
fields.help: Fully-qualified name of the Trino ingestion service in OpenMetadata (e.g. 'trino_prod'). Used as the key to map OM entities to the Ranger Trino service.

fields.name: rangerTagSyncRangerTrinoService
fields.label: Nom du service Ranger Trino
fields.help: Name of the Ranger service of type 'trino' where tags will be applied (e.g. 'cl1_trino').

fields.name: rangerTagSyncComponentType
fields.label: Component table type
fields.help: Value of ranger.tagsync.source.openmetadatarest.component.tabletype. Defaults to 'trino'.

fields.name: rangerTagSyncDownloadInterval
fields.label: Download interval (ms)
fields.help: How often (milliseconds) Ranger TagSync polls OpenMetadata for tag updates.

fields.name: rangerTagSyncBatchSize
fields.label: Batch size (entities)
fields.help: Maximum number of entities fetched per TagSync page request.
